description_long: This is a compact, stylized green airplane with a rounded fuselage and a pair of short, straight wings mounted low on the body. The aircraft features two prominent piston engines, each with a three-bladed propeller, mounted on the wings. The nose is blunt and houses a large air intake, while the cockpit is positioned forward and is enclosed with a clear canopy. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The surface is metallic with visible rivets and panel lines, and the propeller tips are marked with red for visibility.
description: A compact, green, twin-propeller airplane with a rounded fuselage, low-mounted wings, and a forward enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blunt nose, large air intake, forward cockpit canopy, and both propellers with red-tipped blades.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the rounded fuselage, left engine nacelle, cockpit windows, and tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear-front view displaying the wing span, cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical placement of engines and tail surfaces.
description_view_3: Right-side view showing the right engine, cockpit, and the tail's vertical stabilizer.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with short, straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a three-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not visibly extended; likely retractable or fixed, but not clearly shown.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy and visible side windows.
